meaning: n.苏珊娜(Susan 的异体,亦作 Susanna)(f.)
examples: Susannah Fox from the Pew Internet Project says doctors are still the main source of health information.
Susannah Fox says one in five Internet users have gone online to find other people who have the same condition.
Steven Soderbergh and writer Susannah Grant go the later, and hence, paint-by-numbers route.
CNN's Susannah Palk reported this story from London, and Greg Botelho wrote it from Atlanta.
CNN's Susannah Palk in London and Joe Sterling in Atlanta contributed to this report.
